maneuver.name: Disarm
entries: You can use the Disarm maneuver to attempt to knock an item or weapon from a creature's hand.
The target must be within your reach and be holding an item (either carried or wielded). The target drops the item in its space.

maneuver.name: Grab On
entries: While you cannot {@maneuver grapple|sns} a creature two or more size categories larger than you, you can grab onto it. You cling to or balance upon its body, moving into the creature's space where you can move upon its body as if it is difficult terrain.

While grabbing onto a creature, you gain advantage on armour attack rolls against it. The creature may be able to attack you as normal, depending on its limbs or attacks. It can also use an action to try to dislodge you, such as shaking you loose or bashing you against a wall, making a fortitude or reflex attack (your choice) against you. On a success, you take damage equal to the higher of the creature's Strength or Dexterity modifier (minimum 1) and fall {@condition prone} into the nearest space adjacent to it.
If the creature chooses to roll over in order to dislodge you, it becomes {@condition prone}, but its attack to dislodge you has advantage.

maneuver.name: Grapple
entries: You can use the Grapple maneuver to grab or wrestle a creature.

Your target must be within your reach, and it must be no more than one size category larger than you. The target becomes {@condition grappled}. In addition to things that end the condition, you can release the target at any time (no action required).

entries.name: Escaping a Grapple
entries: A creature can use its action to either attempt to escape a grapple or free another {@condition grappled} creature within its reach. It must succeed on a save to free the target.

entries.name: Moving a Grappled Creature
entries: When you move, you can drag or carry the grappled creature with you. When you do so, your Speed is halved, unless the creature's weight is within your carrying capacity - a tiny creature counts as 1 heavy item, small counts as 2, medium as 4, large as 8, huge as 16, and gargantuan as 32.

maneuver.name: Knockdown
entries: You can use the Knockdown maneuver to trip or push a creature down.
The target must be within your reach and be no more than one size category larger than you. You knock the target {@condition prone}.

maneuver.name: Shove
entries: You can use the Shove maneuver to push a creature away.

The target must be within your reach and be no more than one size category larger than you. You push the target 5 feet away from you horizontally. If the triggering attack scored a major success, you push the target an additional 5 feet away, and on a critical success you push the target a further 5 feet away. You can move the creature in any horizontal direction away from you (to the side, forward, or diagonally away).
A creature that is shoved off of a precipice is propelled off of whatever it is standing on — it does not receive any save or ability check to grab onto something to avoid falling down below.

maneuver.name: Cleaving Swing
entries: When you hit with a melee weapon attack using a two-handed weapon, you can use your reaction to deal half the attack's damage against a second creature that is also within your reach.

maneuver.name: Heavy Stance
entries: You gain an expertise die on {@skill Athletics} and {@skill Brawn} checks that use Strength, all incoming combat maneuver attack rolls against you are reduced by {@dice 1d4}, and you ignore the first 10 feet of difficult terrain when you move on your turn.

maneuver.name: Lean Into It
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action.
Until the start of your next turn, when you score a major success against a creature with a melee weapon attack it is knocked {@condition prone}.

maneuver.name: Reactive Knockdown
entries: When a creature within your reach provokes an opportunity attack, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack. On a major success, the creature moves 10 feet in a straight line in the direction it was attempting to move in and is knocked {@condition prone}.

maneuver.name: Shrug It Off
entries: When an effect causes you to be {@condition frightened}, {@condition poisoned}, or {@condition debilitated}, you can use your reaction to prevent it.

maneuver.name: Warding Wield
entries: Until the start of your next turn, your armour increases by 1.

maneuver.name: Battering Strike
entries: When you score a major success against a creature with an attack from a two-handed melee weapon, you can use your reaction knock a weapon it is wielding (your choice) from its grip, causing it to land in a randomly determined space 10 feet away.
You require a critical success on the attack against a creature that is two or more size categories larger than you.

maneuver.name: Crushing Blow
entries: Make a melee weapon attack using a two-handed weapon. On a major success, the target is {@condition staggered} until it takes damage or the end of your next turn (whichever comes first).

maneuver.name: Unbreakable
entries: When you fail a death saving throw, you instead succeed. You can take this reaction even if you are {@condition incapacitated}.

maneuver.name: Stand Tall Stance
entries: You count as being one size category larger.
When you use an Adamant Mountain maneuver that requires a major success on an attack to take effect, it only requires a hit if the target is smaller than you.

maneuver.name: Unstoppable
entries: When an effect causes you to be {@condition staggered} or {@condition debilitated}, you can use your reaction to prevent it. You can take this reaction even if you are {@condition incapacitated}.

maneuver.name: Wild Swing
entries: When you fail to hit with an attack using a two-handed weapon, you can use your reaction to ignore the results of that attack and instead make an attack with the same weapon against another target within your reach.

maneuver.name: Unyielding
entries: When you are reduced to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to regain hit points equal to {@dice 2d12} + your proficiency bonus.
You can take this reaction even if you are {@condition incapacitated}.

maneuver.name: World-Shaking Strike
entries: You hit the ground using a two-handed weapon, sending out a shockwave in a 35-foot cube centered on you. Make a fortitude attack against each other creature in the area, dealing damage as if hit by the weapon and knocking them {@condition prone} on a hit.

maneuver.name: Covering Fire
entries: Choose a number of creatures equal to your proficiency bonus that you can see within your weapon's normal range. Until the start of your next turn, a chosen creature's movement does not provoke opportunity attacks from creatures you can see as you pelt the area nearby with missiles.

maneuver.name: Momentum
entries: The next ranged weapon attack you make deals 2 additional damage against targets for every 30 feet away from you they are.

maneuver.name: Farshot Stance
entries: When you use a weapon to make a ranged attack, increase its normal range by 20 feet and long range by 50 feet.

maneuver.name: Point Blank Shot
entries: Until the end of your next turn, being within 5 feet of a hostile creature who can sense you and who isn't {@condition incapacitated} does not give you disadvantage when making a ranged weapon attack.
In addition, when an adjacent hostile creature that you can see moves 5 feet or more away from you, you can use your reaction to make a ranged weapon attack against it.

maneuver.name: Countershot
entries: When you see a missile from a ranged weapon attack enter into the normal range of a weapon you are wielding, you can use your reaction to deflect it. The attack's success tier is reduced by one.
At the Narrator's discretion, certain ranged weapon attacks (like a giant's thrown rock) may be too heavy to be deflected by Countershot.

maneuver.name: Quickdraw
entries: As long as you are not surprised, when initiative is rolled you can use your reaction to give your initiative roll disadvantage, draw a weapon, and make a ranged attack with that weapon.

maneuver.name: Trickshot
entries: Make a ranged weapon attack with advantage against an unattended object. You can use the result of the attack roll instead of making an {@skill Intimidation} check, and you gain an expertise die when doing so. Otherwise the kinds of trick shots you can make are at the Narrator's discretion. The attack has no other effect.

maneuver.name: Blindshot
entries: Make a {@skill Perception} check contested by the {@skill Stealth} check of a target you cannot sense that is within 30 feet. On a success, you can sense the target during your next ranged attack against it before the end of your turn.

maneuver.name: Missile Volley
entries: Make a ranged weapon attack against a number of creatures in a 5-foot-wide line with length equal to your weapon's range.
You may only target a number of creatures equal to or less than your proficiency bonus and you make a separate attack roll for each target.

maneuver.name: Ricochet
entries: On the next ranged weapon attack you make before the start of your turn, you can completely ignore a target's cover.

Only targets that have total cover from being entirely enclosed by an object or effect (such as inside of a chamber with no openings to shoot through or a sphere from wall of force) have cover against this attack, and you do not have disadvantage from attacking a target you cannot sense.

maneuver.name: Dive For Cover
entries: When you are targeted by a ranged armour attack, after the attack roll is made but before effects are resolved, you can use your reaction to move up to your Speed.

If your movement ends adjacent to either a solid barrier as big as you are or a creature the same size as you or larger, until the beginning of your next turn you have three-quarters cover against ranged armour attacks as long as you remain adjacent to the barrier or creature.

maneuver.name: Heartseeker
entries: Make a ranged weapon attack with advantage against a creature at least 30 feet away from you.
If both attack rolls would hit, it's a critical success. Additionally, if one of the rolls would be a critical success but the other roll would still hit, you deal an additional weapon damage die.

maneuver.name: Mundane Missile Stance
entries: While you are wielding a one-handed weapon, it gains the thrown (20'/60') property.

maneuver.name: Hear the Wind
entries: Until the end of your next turn, you gain blindsight to a range of 20 feet. When you are hit by a ranged attack, you make a disadvantaged {@skill Perception} check against a DC equal to the attack roll.
On a success you reduce the attack's success tier by 1, and on a critical success you ignore it completely and redirect it back at the attacker (using the same attack roll).

maneuver.name: Horizon Shot
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action. Choose one target. Until the start of your next turn, the target is considered to be within your weapon's normal range and it gains no benefit from cover less than total cover.

maneuver.name: Knockdown Assault
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action. Until the start of your next turn, when you score a major success with a melee weapon attack against a creature and deal 8 or more damage it is knocked {@condition prone}.

maneuver.name: Leading Throw
entries: When a creature within your reach fails to hit you with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to try to throw it. Make a reflex attack against the creature with the following outcomes:

items.name: Minor success
entries: The target is pushed 5 feet in a horizontal direction of your choice.

items.name: Standard success
entries: As minor success, but increased to 15 feet.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As minor success, but increased to 20 feet.

maneuver.name: Warning Strike
entries: When a hostile creature moves out of a space adjacent to you, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack and on a hit its Speed is reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn.

maneuver.name: Wary Stance
entries: You gain an expertise die on {@skill Insight} checks and increase your passive {@skill Insight} score by 3.

maneuver.name: Assisted Roll
entries: When a creature within your reach fails to hit you with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to shift 15 feet after the attack's effects are resolved.

maneuver.name: Off-Balancing Strikes
entries: Until the start of your next turn, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, instead of dealing damage and triggering major effects you can choose to use pulls, shoves, and strikes to put your target off-balance.
Until the end of your next turn, the target is {@condition sluggish}, it has disadvantage on ability checks, and reflex attacks against it have advantage.

maneuver.name: Take Weapon
entries: When a creature fails to hit you with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to flip the weapon out of the creature's hands after the attack's effects have been resolved. Make a reflex attack against the creature with the following outcomes: The creature makes a Dexterity saving throw, and on a failure it drops the weapon and you grab it from mid-air.

items.name: Minor success
entries: The target drops the weapon.

items.name: Standard success
entries: As minor success, and you grab the weapon from mid-air, stowing one of your held items at the same time so that you can hold it.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As standard success, and the exertion cost to immediately deal damage to the target (see below) is reduced to 1.
If the attack hit, you can spend 2 exertion points to immediately have the creature take half of the weapon's damage.

maneuver.name: Flowing Form
entries: You take the Dodge action. Until the start of your next turn you make one melee weapon attack against each target that fails to hit you with a melee weapon attack, and you have disadvantage on {@skill Perception} checks.

maneuver.name: Redirect
entries: When you are hit with a melee weapon attack from a Large or smaller creature, you can use your reaction to reduce the attack's success tier by one against you, and also cause the difference in effect that this tier reduction made to be applied to another creature adjacent to you.
Once you have used this maneuver against a creature, it has no effect against that creature for the remainder of combat.

maneuver.name: Discerning Strike
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action.
Until the start of your next turn, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack you learn its current hit points, hit point maximum, immunities, resistances, and vulnerabilities.

If you score a critical hit you can choose one of the creature's resistances or immunities. The creature loses that resistance or immunity until the end of your next turn. In addition, until the beginning of your next turn you gain advantage on melee weapon attacks against that creature.

maneuver.name: Retributive Blow
entries: When you are hit with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against the attacker. On a hit, that creature has disadvantage on armour attack rolls against you until the beginning of your next turn.

maneuver.name: Strike the Cracks Stance
entries: Your critical hit range for melee weapon attacks is increased by 1.
You deal one additional weapon damage die when determining the extra damage for a critical hit with a melee weapon attack.

maneuver.name: Blinding Strikes
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action.
Until the start of your next turn, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, it is {@condition blinded} and can't benefit from either blindsight or tremorsense.
At the beginning of each of its turns, a creature {@condition blinded} by this maneuver can make a save, ending the effect on itself on a success.

maneuver.name: Reflect Attack
entries: When you are hit with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to redirect the force back to the attacker. The attack's success tier is reduce by one and the attacker takes the difference in effect that this tier reduction made.
If you have already hit that creature with a Mirror's Glint maneuver since the beginning of your last turn, the attack's success tier is reduced by two instead.

maneuver.name: Anticipate Spell
entries: Until the start of your next turn, when you see a creature within 5 feet of you cast a spell you can use your reaction to make either an opportunity attack or a {@skill Finesse} check against it.

maneuver.name: Deceptive Stance
entries: You gain an expertise die on {@skill Deception} and {@skill Finesse} checks made in combat.

maneuver.name: Painful Pickpocket
entries: When a creature hits you with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to make a {@skill Finesse} check against it.

maneuver.name: Perplexing Flurry
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action. As long as your attack is a melee weapon attack, the target has disadvantage on armour attack rolls against you until the start of your next turn.

maneuver.name: Agile Feint
entries: Choose a creature within your reach. You have advantage on your next weapon attack roll against it.

maneuver.name: Force Hesitation
entries: Make a reflex attack against a creature within your reach with the following outcomes. On a failure, it reacts to a strike from you that never comes. As long as you remain within the target's reach, until the start of your next turn it cannot attack any creature other than you.

items.name: Minor success
entries: As long as your remain within its reach, the target has disadvantage on armour attacks against creatures other than you until the start of your next turn.

items.name: Standard success
entries: As long as your remain within its reach, the target cannot make armour attacks against creatures other than you until the start of your next turn.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As long as you remain within its reach, the target cannot make attacks against creatures other than you until the start of your next turn.
After you have used this maneuver against a creature, its attack has disadvantage against that creature until the end of combat.

maneuver.name: Mugging Hit
entries: Choose a creature you can see. On a hit with your next melee weapon attack against it before the start of your next turn, you can also make a {@skill Finesse} check against the creature.

maneuver.name: Douse
entries: You splash a held container of liquid, such as a contact poison, holy water, or alchemical substance, at a target within 20 feet.
This maneuver can also be used to extinguish a small nonmagical fire in a 5-foot-square area.

maneuver.name: Feinting Strike
entries: Make a melee weapon attack using the result of a disadvantaged {@skill Finesse} check instead of your usual attack roll.

maneuver.name: Pickpocket
entries: When a creature is within your reach, you can use your reaction to make a {@skill Finesse} check against it.

maneuver.name: Blinding Blow Stance
entries: After you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can spend 1 exertion to make a {@skill Finesse} check. Reduce the result by the target's armour, and if it succeeds against a DC of 15, the target is {@condition blinded}.
At the beginning of each of its turns, the {@condition blinded} creature can make a save to recover from its blindness.

maneuver.name: Spinning Parry
entries: You can use your reaction to reduce the success tier of a creature's makes a melee weapon attack against you by one.
In addition, you may also swap places with the attacker (no opportunity attacks provoked), then make a will attack against it with the following outcomes:

items.name: Minor success
entries: The target has disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of its next turn.

items.name: Standard success
entries: The target is {@condition staggered} until the start of its next turn.

items.name: Critical success
entries: The target is {@condition staggered} until the end of its next turn.

maneuver.name: Steal Momentum
entries: When a creature moves into your reach you can use your reaction to make a {@skill Finesse} check. Reduce the result by the creature's armour, and if it succeeds against a DC of 15, the creature is {@condition restrained} until the start of its next turn.

maneuver.name: Armour Lock
entries: Make a {@skill Finesse} check. Reduce the result by the armour of a creature within reach that is wearing armour, and if it succeeds against a DC of 15, the creature is {@condition staggered}. At the beginning of each of its turns, a creature {@condition staggered} by this maneuver can make a save, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Your {@skill Finesse} check has advantage if the creature is wearing heavy armour.

maneuver.name: Pilfer Object
entries: When a creature within 15 feet draws an item, you can use your reaction to shift into a space adjacent to it and make a will attack against it with the following outcomes:

items.name: Minor success
entries: The item is knocked from the target's hand, dropping onto the ground in its space.

items.name: Standard success
entries: You take the item for yourself, stowing one of your held items at the same time so you can hold it.

items.name: Critical success
entries: You take the item for yourself and immediately use it, stowing one of your held items at the same time so you can hold it.

maneuver.name: Charge
entries: Move up to 30 feet in a straight line and make a melee weapon attack. You can't take the Dash action and use this maneuver on the same turn.

maneuver.name: Eye Slash
entries: Make a melee weapon attack against a creature. On a major success, it is {@condition blinded} until the end of your next turn.

maneuver.name: Speed Over Strength
entries: When a creature attacks you with a two-handed melee weapon, or a Large-sized or larger creature attacks you with a natural weapon, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack so long as either your main hand weapon is Dexterity-based or you are holding a weapon with the offhand trait.

maneuver.name: Swift Stance
entries: Your Speed increases by 5 feet.

maneuver.name: Parrying Counter
entries: At the end of your turn, you can use your reaction to add an expertise die to your armour against one creature you can see within reach.
This increase to your armour only applies to the chosen creature and ends at the start of your next turn.
If the creature fails to hit you with one or more armour attacks before the start of your next turn, you can make a melee weapon attack against it.

maneuver.name: Rapid Drink
entries: You drink a potion or administer a potion to a creature within reach.

maneuver.name: Rolling Strike
entries: Shift up to 20 feet and make a melee weapon attack.

maneuver.name: Disarming Counter
entries: When a creature makes a melee weapon attack against you and fails to hit, you can use your reaction to make a reflex attack against it with the following outcomes:

items.name: Minor success
entries: The target drops your choice of a weapon it is wielding in its space.

items.name: Standard success
entries: As minor success, but the weapon lands in a randomly-determined space 10 feet away.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As standard success, but the space is 15 feet away.

maneuver.name: Rapid Strike
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action.
After hitting a creature with a melee weapon attack as part of this action, you can use your bonus action to increase the attack's damage by the weapon's damage.
Alternatively, if you expend an additional 2 exertion points, you can increase the attack's damage by the weapon's damage twice.

maneuver.name: Whirlwind Strike
entries: Make a melee weapon attack against any number of creatures within 5 feet of you, making a separate attack roll for each target. You may only target a number of creatures equal to or less than your proficiency bonus.

maneuver.name: First Blood
entries: After rolling initiative, you can use your reaction to treat your initiative as if you had rolled a critical success.

maneuver.name: Flowing Steps Stance
entries: You allow your movements to adapt to all conditions. Whenever you would become {@condition grappled}, {@condition staggered}, {@condition restrained}, or {@condition debilitated}, make a save, ignoring the condition on a success. In addition, you ignore difficult terrain.

maneuver.name: Whirlpool Strike
entries: Make a melee weapon attack against any number of creatures within 5 feet of you, making a separate attack roll for each target. For each subsequent hit after the first, you deal an additional {@dice 1d6} damage.

maneuver.name: Tidal Parry
entries: When a creature hits you with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to attempt to evade the attack.
Make a reflex attack against the creature, with the following outcomes:

items.name: Minor success
entries: The target's attack becomes a standard success. If it already was one, you reduce its damage by your proficiency bonus.

items.name: Standard success
entries: The target's attack becomes a minor success.

items.name: Critical success
entries: The target's attack becomes a critical failure.
You have disadvantage on this attack if the triggering attack scored a critical success.

maneuver.name: Tsunami Dash
entries: You take the Dash action. During your turn, any time you become adjacent to a creature, you can make a melee attack against it using a weapon that has the Offhand trait or is Dexterity-based.
You cannot attack the same creature twice in a row in this manner or make more attacks than your proficiency bonus.

maneuver.name: Dangerous Strikes
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action. Until the start of your next turn, your weapon attacks have +1 critical success range.

maneuver.name: Exploit Footing
entries: When a creature makes a melee weapon attack against you with advantage and misses, or when it has disadvantage and both attack rolls would miss, you can use your reaction to trip it. Make a reflex attack against the creature, with the following outcomes:

items.name: Minor success
entries: The target is {@condition sluggish} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Standard success
entries: The target is knocked {@condition prone}.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As standard success, and the target is also {@condition sluggish} until the end of its next turn.

maneuver.name: Iron Will
entries: When you would become {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}, you can use your reaction to make a save, ignoring the condition on a success.

maneuver.name: Perceptive Stance
entries: Your passive {@skill Perception} score increases by 3. In addition, you gain an expertise die on {@skill Perception} checks.

maneuver.name: Practiced Roll
entries: Shift 20 feet.

maneuver.name: Twist the Blade
entries: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack within 2 of your critical success range, you can use your reaction to turn the attack into a critical success.

maneuver.name: Use The Pain
entries: When a creature scores a critical success against you with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against it.

maneuver.name: Drive Back
entries: Make a melee weapon attack against a creature. Whether or not you hit, the creature is pushed back 10 feet.

maneuver.name: Instinctive Counterattack
entries: At any point outside of your turn, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack.

maneuver.name: Mind Over Body
entries: Until the start of your next turn, gain 2 damage resistance.

maneuver.name: Dashing Razor
entries: Move up to your Speed and make a melee weapon attack against a creature. On a hit, for the next minute your Speed is increased by 10 feet.

maneuver.name: Perfect Edge Stance
entries: Your weapon attacks have +2 critical success range.

maneuver.name: Sharpened Awareness
entries: You can use your reaction to make a disadvantaged {@skill Perception} check when a creature makes an armour attack against you, turning it into a minor success if your result is higher than the attack roll. In addition, if the attack is turned into a minor success you gain advantage on armour attacks made against that creature until the end of your next turn.
If the attack still hits you, the creature has disadvantage on its next armour attack made against you before the end of its next turn.

maneuver.name: Death Blow
prerequisites: Two instances of the Empowered Attack feature
entries: Make a melee weapon attack against a creature.
On a hit, your attack becomes a critical success, and if your attack would have already been a critical success the creature makes a save.
On a failure, the creature is reduced to 0 hit points.

maneuver.name: Heightened Concentration
entries: When you lose concentration, you can use your reaction to prevent yourself from losing concentration.

maneuver.name: Doubleteam
entries: Choose a creature within your reach. The next ally within 20 feet that makes a melee weapon attack against that creature can use their bonus action to force the target to make a save, becoming {@condition grappled} by them or being pushed 10 feet on a failure.

maneuver.name: Legion Stance
entries: When you score a critical success with an armour attack against a creature, an ally within reach of the same creature can use their reaction to make an opportunity attack against it.

maneuver.name: Shield Wall
entries: While you are wielding a shield, you can choose one ally within 5 feet that is also wielding a shield.
Until the start of your next turn, as long as that ally remains within 5 feet of you and is able to take actions both you and your ally increase your armour by 1.

maneuver.name: Shoulder Check
entries: After a creature within your reach makes a melee weapon attack against an ally, you can use your reaction to make a fortitude or reflex attack (target's choice) against the creature, using a disadvantaged {@skill Athletics} check as the attack roll, with the following results.On a success you shove the creature 10 feet away from you and you move 10 feet in that direction.

items.name: Minor success
entries: The target is pushed 5 feet away from you and you immediately move 5 feet in the same direction.

items.name: Standard success
entries: As minor success, but all distances are increased to 10 feet and your movement becomes a shift.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As standard success, but all distances are increased to 15 feet.

maneuver.name: Back To Back
entries: Choose an ally adjacent to you. Until the start of your next turn, as long as that ally remains adjacent to you and is able to take actions, and both of you are unmounted, you and your ally increase your armour by 2.
In addition, each of you gets an extra reaction that can be used before the start of your next turn to activate a combat maneuver that cannot make attacks.

maneuver.name: Double Tackle
entries: Choose an ally you can see. Your ally can use their reaction to move up to 20 feet to be adjacent to a creature you designate.

As long as your ally moved at least 20 feet and both you and your ally are adjacent to the creature, you make a fortitude attack against the creature with the following outcomes:it makes a Strength saving throw or is knocked {@condition prone}.

items.name: Minor success
entries: The target is {@condition sluggish} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Standard success
entries: The target is knocked {@condition prone}.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As standard success, and the target is also {@condition sluggish} until the end of its next turn.

Alternatively before you make the attack, you and your ally may both choose to go {@condition prone} to force attack's result to be a standard success. You cannot use this maneuver against creatures more than one size category larger than you or your ally.

maneuver.name: Dual Grapple
entries: A creature an ally has {@condition grappled} makes a save. On a failure, the creature is {@condition restrained} until the grapple ends, and either you or your ally (your choice) are also {@condition restrained} until the grapple ends.

maneuver.name: Follow-Up Topple
entries: When an ally within your reach hits a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature. On a major success, the creature is knocked {@condition prone}.

maneuver.name: Hurl Ally
entries: Choose a willing ally within your reach that is at least one size category smaller than you. You hurl your ally at a creature within 20 feet. Your ally lands in a space adjacent to the creature and can use their reaction to make a melee weapon attack against it. On a major success, the attack becomes a critical success (or if the attack is already a critical success your ally deals maximum damage).

maneuver.name: Look At Me!
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action. Until the start of your next turn, when you make a melee weapon attack against a creature you can choose one ally within reach.
The chosen ally does not provoke opportunity attacks from that creature until the start of your next turn.
In addition, the next melee weapon attack the chosen ally makes against that creature before the end of their next turn gains an expertise die.

maneuver.name: Bodyguard
entries: When an ally within your reach is hit by an armour attack, you can use your reaction to swap places with them and become the target of that attack, taking half as much damage as normal.

maneuver.name: Brotherhood Stance
entries: Whenever you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can also take the Help action.
On an armour attack critical success, the target of your Help action can use their reaction to take the Dodge action.

maneuver.name: Doubletime
entries: You take the Disengage action, and up to 3 allies within your reach can use their reactions to shift up to their Speed.

maneuver.name: Rallying Cry
entries: When an ally you can see takes damage from an armour attack, you can use your reaction to release a hearty rallying cry. As long as they are able to hear you, your ally regains an amount of hit points equal to triple your proficiency bonus.

maneuver.name: United We Stand
entries: Make a melee weapon attack against a creature. Any willing ally within your reach can use their reaction to make a melee weapon attack against the same creature.
If the creature is hit by two attacks, it takes extra damage from your attack equal to your proficiency bonus.
If the creature is hit by three attacks, it takes extra damage from your attack equal to twice your proficiency bonus and it is knocked {@condition prone}.

If the creature is hit by four or more attacks, it takes extra damage from your attack equal to twice your proficiency bonus, it is knocked {@condition prone}, and it is {@condition debilitated} until the end of its next turn.

maneuver.name: Cavalier Stance
entries: While you are riding a mount, your armour increases by 1.

maneuver.name: Lancer Strike
entries: You must be wielding a weapon with the Reach trait, mounted, and have displaced 20 or more feet this turn in order to use this maneuver.
Make a melee weapon attack against a creature within your weapon's reach. On a hit, you deal an extra {@dice 1d6} damage and on a substantial success, the creature is knocked {@condition prone}.

maneuver.name: Mounted Charge
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action.
You must be mounted to use this maneuver.
Move up to your mount's Speed in a straight line. If at the end of your movement there is a creature within your reach, the attack you make against it as part of this maneuver has advantage.
You can't take the Dash action and use this maneuver on the same turn.

maneuver.name: Riding Leap
entries: As long as you are mounted and move at least 30 feet in a straight line first, your mount can jump further and higher than usual. The height of the jump increases by a number of feet equal to your proficiency bonus and the distance of the jump increases by a number of feet equal to twice your proficiency bonus.
Your movement this turn counts as shifting.

maneuver.name: Rearing Menace
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action.
You must be mounted to use this maneuver.
Make a will attack against a number of creatures equal to your proficiency bonus that are within 20 feet, with the following outcomes:

items.name: Minor success
entries: The target is {@condition sluggish} while moving towards you until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Standard success
entries: The target is {@condition frightened} until the end of your next turn.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As standard success, and the target is also {@condition debilitated} for the same duration.
These attacks have disadvantage against creatures of the same size as your mount or larger.

maneuver.name: Spur Mount
entries: While you are riding your mount and you make an ability check, you can use your reaction to grant it advantage, and when you are targeted with a non-armour attack you can use your reaction to grant it disadvantage.

maneuver.name: Trample
entries: You must be mounted to use this maneuver.

Shift up to half your Speed. Creatures in your path that are at least one size smaller than your mount make a save. On a failure, a creature immediately suffers {@dice 2d6} bludgeoning harm and is knocked {@condition prone}.

maneuver.name: Launched Strike
entries: You must be mounted to use this maneuver.
You leap 10 feet away from your mount and make a melee weapon attack against a creature within your reach before you land. On a hit, your attack becomes a critical success.

maneuver.name: Sacrifice Mount
entries: You must be mounted to use this maneuver.
When a creature targets you with an attack you can use your reaction to swing down the side of your mount, automatically causing the attack to score a critical failure, but dismounting you.

maneuver.name: Saddled Blows
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action.
You must be mounted to use this maneuver.

Until the end of your next turn, when you use a melee weapon attack and score a major success against a creature of a size smaller than your mount, it suffers one of the following (your choice): {@condition confused} until the start of your next turn, knocked {@condition prone}, or {@condition slowed} until the end of your next turn.

maneuver.name: Prodigious Leap
entries: When your mount makes an {@skill Athletics} check to jump, you can use your reaction to grant it a fly speed equal to half its Speed until the end of your turn. Your mount can only fly in a straight line.

maneuver.name: Spirited Whistle
entries: When you have become separated from your mount but can see it, you can use an action to whistle. As long as your mount is able to hear you, it uses its reaction to move until it is adjacent to you.
Your mount suffers a level of fatigue if this movement is more than twice its Speed.

maneuver.name: Steely Steed Stance
entries: When you make a melee weapon attack against a creature while mounted, you gain advantage on armour attack rolls against that creature until the end of your next turn.
In addition, while mounted, incoming non-armour attacks against you have disadvantage and you gain 50% resistance to all damage.

maneuver.name: Reassuring Pat
entries: When you are hit by an armour attack while mounted, you can use your reaction to pat your mount reassuringly. You regain hit points equal to your level plus your proficiency bonus.
If you are {@condition charmed}, {@condition poisoned}, or {@condition restrained}, you can end those conditions.

maneuver.name: Wheeling Charge
entries: You take the Dodge action. If your mount moves at least 10 feet in a straight line this turn and there's a creature within your reach, make a melee attack against that creature.

If you can continue your movement for at least 10 more feet, then return, you can turn around and increase the attack's damage by your weapon's damage. You can do this any number of times as per your mount's movement.
You must end your movement at least 10 feet away from the creature.

maneuver.name: Imposing Glare
entries: Choose one hostile creature you can sense within 30 feet. If it can sense you, it makes a save or is {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn.
After you have used this maneuver against a creature, until the end of combat it has advantage on saves to resist Imposing Glare.

maneuver.name: Purge Magic
entries: When a creature within your reach casts a spell, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against it.

maneuver.name: Striding Swings
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action. Throughout this action, you may move up to 15 feet. This movement can be through the space of hostile creatures that are up to one size category larger than you.

maneuver.name: Zealous Stance
entries: When you make a melee weapon attack on your turn that is either utilising a weapon with the Offhand trait or affected by the Empowered Attack feature, you can decide to gain an expertise die on that attack roll. When you do so, until the start of your next turn armour attack rolls against you gain an expertise die.

maneuver.name: Faith Within
entries: Whenever magic causes you to become {@condition charmed}, {@condition frightened}, {@condition poisoned}, or {@condition debilitated}, at the start of your next turn you can use your reaction to make a save, removing the condition on a success.

maneuver.name: Gaze Of Conviction
entries: You stare down a creature you can sense within 30 feet, and if it can sense you it makes a save. On a failure, the creature is compelled to attack you. On its turns the creature uses its movement to move towards you and then takes the Attack action against you.
The effects of this maneuver end when you attack a different creature or the creature is unable to attack you.

maneuver.name: Stunning Assault
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action.
Until the start of your next turn, when you score a major success with a melee weapon attack the target is {@condition debilitated} until the end of your next turn.

maneuver.name: Break Spell
entries: When a creature within your reach casts a spell, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against it. On a major success, the target does not cast that spell (and therefore does not expend any resources on it, including any actions) and cannot cast that spell for the next minute.

maneuver.name: Defy Magic
entries: When a creature within 30 feet makes a spell armour attack against you, you can use your reaction to defy it, reducing the attack's success tier by 1.
If the spell is of 7th-level or higher, this combat maneuver has no effect.

maneuver.name: Dispelling Assault
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action. Until the start of your next turn, your weapon attacks have +1 critical success range, and when you score a critical hit against a creature any spell of 3rd-level or lower on the creature ends.

maneuver.name: Devoted Assault
entries: Choose one creature you can sense within 30 feet. Until the start of your next turn, you cannot attack any other targets and you have advantage on weapon attacks made against that creature.

On a critical hit within this duration, you can spend exertion to use a Tempered Iron maneuver that you know against the creature so long as it can be activated with an action or bonus action and cannot make attacks.

maneuver.name: Disrupting Charge
entries: When a creature within your line of sight casts a spell, you can use your reaction to move up to your Speed. If you end your movement adjacent to that creature, you can make a melee weapon attack against it. On a critical success, the target does not cast that spell (and therefore does not expend any resources on it, including any actions) and cannot cast that spell for the next minute.

maneuver.name: Spell Shattering Strike
entries: When a creature within your reach casts a spell, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against it. On a major success, the target does not cast that spell (and therefore does not expend any resources on it, including any actions) and cannot cast that spell for the next minute.
Until the end of its next turn, the creature's Speed is reduced to 0 while you are adjacent to it, otherwise it is {@condition sluggish}.
The creature becomes {@condition confused} until the end of its next turn.
The next time the creature takes damage while concentrating, they must make a save, losing concentration on a failure.
The creature is {@condition debilitated} until the end of its next turn and {@condition rattled} for the next minute.

maneuver.name: Branding Steel
entries: Before the start of your next turn, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack you can choose to brand it. A branded creature has disadvantage on armour attacks made against creatures other than you, and whenever it takes damage while concentrating, it must make a save, losing concentration on a failure.

In addition, a branded creature cannot move closer to you without passing a save (only one save is made each turn). The brand on a creature is removed when it regains 30 hit points or more from a healing spell or similar magic.

maneuver.name: Burning Embers of Faith
entries: When you are reduced to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to heal a number of hit points equal to double your proficiency bonus.

If you were reduced to 0 hit points by a spell, you instead heal a number of hit points equal to your level plus your proficiency bonus. In addition, for the next minute you gain advantage on armour attack rolls made against the creature that reduced you to 0 hit points, and your weapon attacks against it deal extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus.

maneuver.name: Bounding Strike
entries: Move 15 feet in a straight line and make a disadvantaged {@skill Finesse} or {@skill Athletics} check to jump as you do so. If a creature is within your reach when you land, you can make a melee weapon attack against it using the result of your check instead of an attack roll.

maneuver.name: Raking Strike
entries: Make a melee weapon attack. On a hit, the attack deals additional damage equal to its governing ability modifier.

maneuver.name: Ride Enemy
entries: When a creature at least one size category larger than you fails to hit you with a natural weapon attack, you can use your reaction to use the {@maneuver Grab On|sns} basic maneuver against it.

maneuver.name: Springing Stance
entries: You increase the distance of your horizontal jumps by a number of feet equal to your proficiency bonus, and the distance of your vertical jumps by a number of feet equal to half your proficiency bonus. In addition, you gain an expertise die on {@skill Finesse} checks and checks made to jump. This affects your journey activity checks.

maneuver.name: Expert Tumble
entries: As long as you move your full Speed before the end of your turn (and your Speed is greater than 0), your armour increases by an amount equal to your proficiency bonus until the start of your next turn.

maneuver.name: Leaping Strike
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action. During this action, you may move up to 40 feet in a straight line. If you score a major success with a melee weapon attack at the end of your movement, the target is either knocked {@condition prone} or pushed 10 feet away from you (your choice).

maneuver.name: Primal Intercept
entries: When a creature makes a melee weapon attack against or grapples you, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against it.

maneuver.name: Gut Strike
entries: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can use your reaction to give the target one level of {@condition fatigue} for 1 minute. If the weapon attack scored a critical success, the target gains two levels of {@condition fatigue} instead.
A target cannot gain more than two levels of fatigue from this maneuver.

maneuver.name: Rake
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action. The attack deals {@dice 1d8} extra base damage for each instance of the Empowered Attack feature affecting it.

maneuver.name: Wild Capering
entries: Move 30 feet. Until the start of your next turn, you gain a climb speed equal to your Speed and your minimum jump distance increases to 10 feet vertically and 15 feet horizontally.

maneuver.name: Blind Instinct
entries: You gain blindsight out to a range of 30 feet.

If a creature leaves the range of your blindsight, you can use your reaction to mark it as prey and move up to your Speed. The next melee weapon attack you make against that creature before the end of your next turn deals additional damage equal to the attack's base weapon damage die.

maneuver.name: Bloody Roar
entries: Make a melee weapon attack against a creature.

On a major success, you release a mighty roar that shakes the very earth and firmament in a 30-foot cube centered on you. Each hostile creature in the area suffers psychic harm equal to twice your proficiency bonus and becomes {@condition frightened} until the end of its next turn. On a standard success, the same creatures suffer half as much psychic harm and do not become {@condition frightened}.

maneuver.name: Mercurial Striking Stance
entries: The minimum result on a damage die rolled with a natural weapon, unarmed strike, or weapon being dual-wielded is a 3.

maneuver.name: Furious Blow
entries: Make a melee weapon attack against a creature.
On a hit, make a save. Do this again until you fail a save, or you have succeeded on a number of saves equal to your proficiency bonus.
For each save you succeeded, the attack's damage is increased by your weapon's base damage.
Armour attack rolls against you have advantage until the beginning of your next turn.

maneuver.name: Wounded Animal Gambit
entries: When you lose hit points, you can use your reaction to fall {@condition prone} and pretend to be dead. All hostile creatures able to see you consider you to be dead or dying.
If a creature moves within your reach before the beginning of your next turn, you can immediately stand up and spend exertion to use a Tooth and Claw maneuver you know against it.
No action is required to activate the maneuver and any melee weapon attack made as part of the maneuver has advantage.
After you have used Wounded Animal Gambit against a creature, if you activate it again before the end of combat that creature makes a save, realising you are playing dead on a success.

maneuver.name: Dangerous Signature
entries: Choose a simple symbol (such as a letter) to be your signature. When you have advantage on a melee weapon attack roll, on a hit you can leave your signature on the creature or object you attacked. Your signature remains on a creature until it has taken a long rest.
While a creature bears your signature, you can use a bonus action to try to intimidate it.
The creature makes a save or becomes {@condition frightened} of you until the start of your next turn.

maneuver.name: Throwing Stance
entries: Choose a weapon when you learn this combat maneuver. While you are wielding the chosen weapon it gains the Thrown (20'/60') trait.

maneuver.name: Victory Pose
entries: Choose a weapon when you learn this combat maneuver. When you score a critical hit with this weapon, you can use your reaction to pose dramatically as you finish the strike.

As you dramatically pose, choose a number of allies equal to your proficiency bonus within 30 feet. The chosen allies have advantage on the next death saving throw each makes before the end of combat.

maneuver.name: Wounding Strike
entries: Choose a weapon when you learn this combat maneuver. If you hit with your next attack roll using the chosen weapon against a living creature, you deliver a wound that causes {@dice 1d4} ongoing harm of the weapon's base type that lasts until the creature succeeds on a passive save.
Alternatively, the wounded creature, or a creature adjacent to it, can use an action to make a {@skill Medicine} check against your standard DC, ending the ongoing harm on a success.
The effect also ends if the wounded creature recovers any number of hit points.

maneuver.name: Deflect Strike
entries: When a creature hits you with a weapon armour attack, you can use your reaction to reduce the attack's damage by a number of {@dice d6} equal to your proficiency bonus.
If this maneuver reduces the damage to 0, the attack is no longer considered a hit.

maneuver.name: Instant Strike
entries: Choose a creature within your reach. You draw a melee weapon that you are not currently holding and use it to cause harm equal to the weapon's base damage to that creature. You cannot use this combat maneuver against the same creature more than once per combat.

maneuver.name: Preternatural Strikes
entries: Choose a weapon when you learn this combat maneuver. When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action. Until the start of your next turn, your weapon attacks using that weapon ignore all damage resistance.

maneuver.name: Disarming Assault
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action.

Until the start of your next turn, your strikes can deprive foes of their armaments. In addition to dealing damage, when you score a major success against a creature with a melee weapon attack, an item of your choice that the creature is holding flies from its grip to land in a randomly determined space 10 feet away.

maneuver.name: Expert Sidestep
entries: When a creature makes a weapon attack against you, you can use your reaction to reduce the attack's success tier by 1.

maneuver.name: Unsettling Injury
entries: Choose a weapon when you learn this combat maneuver. Make a melee weapon attack using the chosen weapon, and on a major success the target loses concentration on any spell it has cast, any combat stance it is using ends, and it cannot use combat maneuvers or cast spells until the end of your next turn.

maneuver.name: Any Weapon Stance
entries: You gain proficiency with all weapons, and the damage die size of all weapons is increased by 1 for you.

maneuver.name: Mistaken Opportunity
entries: When you miss with a melee weapon attack against a creature, you can use your reaction to ignore the results of that attack to take your momentum and target a different creature within your reach with a melee attack using the same weapon.
This attack is made with advantage and deals extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus.
If both attack rolls would hit the creature, it is {@condition debilitated} until the end of its next turn.

maneuver.name: Wind Strike
entries: Choose a melee weapon when you learn this combat maneuver. You can use the chosen weapon to make a ranged attack against a distant target with air pressure, giving your attack a normal range of 20 feet and long range of 60 feet. This functions mechanically as if your weapon had the Thrown trait, except you don't actually throw it and it does not count as a thrown attack.
You have advantage on attack rolls made using this maneuver. If both attack rolls hit, you deal an additional weapon damage die.

After using this maneuver to hit a creature, it can use an action to make a {@skill Perception} check against your standard DC. On a success, for the next minute any uses of Wind Strike do not grant advantage on attack rolls made against the creature.

maneuver.name: Heart of the Sword
entries: Choose a weapon when you learn this combat maneuver. For the next minute, attacks made with the chosen weapon have +1 critical success range and ignore any damage resistances a target might have.

maneuver.name: Perfect Assault
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action. Before or after the action's attack, you may use a combat maneuver you know that cannot make attacks. At the end of your turn your exertion pool is reduced to 0 and you suffer a level of fatigue.

maneuver.name: Blackguard's Blight
entries: Before the start of your next turn, when you score a major success against a creature with a melee weapon attack you can choose to lay a curse on it. The cursed target is unable to gain advantage on armour attack rolls against you for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier. This curse can only be applied to one creature at a time.

maneuver.name: Darkstalker Stance
entries: Choose a number of 5-foot spaces equal to your Charisma modifier within 30 feet. Each space becomes occupied by a spirit entity with Armour equal to half your Charisma modifier, 22 hit points, and vulnerability to radiant damage. Each entity also has a reach of 5 feet and its own reaction. When a creature moves out of its reach, an entity can react to cause half of your main hand weapon's base damage as harm to the creature if your main hand weapon is a melee weapon. The entities cannot use any movement and cannot flank. The spaces they occupy are considered difficult terrain to any creature except you.

Additionally, while this stance is active, whenever you take the Attack action, you can make your attack from a chosen entity instead of from you. This stance ends early if all entities are destroyed.

maneuver.name: Deathgrip
entries: Make a melee weapon attack. On a major success, the target takes additional necrotic damage equal to your Strength modifier and becomes {@condition restrained} until the beginning of your next turn.

maneuver.name: Dreadful Edge
entries: Make a melee weapon attack. On a major success, the target is {@condition rattled} for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier.

maneuver.name: Fell Spines
entries: Choose a number of targets up to your proficiency bonus in a 15-foot cone. Make an area weapon reflex attack against each that deals damage equal to your Strength modifier. The target is also {@condition rattled} until the start of its next turn. This attack's damage is considered both necrotic and piercing.

maneuver.name: Grasping Shadows
entries: Choose 4 5-foot spaces within 20 feet of you that are in dim light or darkness. These spaces become areas of magical darkness which are treated as difficult terrain and can't be illuminated except by magic, such as the {@spell light|sns} spell. This effect lasts until the end of your next turn.

maneuver.name: Green-Flame Strike
entries: As a bonus action, you coat your weapon in green flame, causing your next melee weapon attack before the end of your next turn to potentially damage an additional target. The attack's original target receives the effects of the attack as normal, but if the attack scores a major success, an additional target of your choice within reach suffers necrotic harm equal to half the damage dealt to the first target.

maneuver.name: Hungry Ghost Stance
entries: Choose a number of 5-foot spaces equal to your Charisma modifier within 30 feet. These spaces become occupied with shadowy apparitions, which have Armour equal to half your Charisma modifier and 11 hit points. Each apparition also has a reach of 5 feet and its own reaction. When a creature moves out of its reach, an apparition can react to cause necrotic harm equal to half of your Charisma modifier to the creature, and you gain hit points equal to the harm caused. You can also use your own reaction to direct an apparition to cause this harm again if it has already used its own. The apparitions cannot flank.

While maintaining this stance, you can forgo your movement to instead move any apparition a number of feet up to your Speed. This can be split among multiple apparitions, as long as you have the movement available. The apparitions must stay within 30 feet of you or they vanish. This stance ends early if all apparitions are destroyed.

maneuver.name: Life Drinker
entries: Make a melee weapon attack. If this attack scores a major success against a creature, you gain hit points equal to one of the target's hit dice + your Fortitude (two hit dice one a critical success). This does not deal additional damage, but does expend the hit die if the creature is capable of spending them.

maneuver.name: Living Shadow
entries: Choose a space within 15 feet. Until the start of your next turn, your shadow (0 Armour, 2 HP) occupied the chosen space and has a reach of 5 feet. It cannot attack, but it can serve as a flanking partner for you and one other creature that you designate when using this maneuver.

maneuver.name: Mistfade Retreat
entries: When you are hit by a melee weapon attack, you can shift 15 feet.

maneuver.name: Necrotic Grip
entries: When you are grappling an opponent, you can use your reaction to cause necrotic harm equal to {@dice 1d8} + your Charisma modifier.

maneuver.name: Shadowy Feint
entries: After hitting with a melee weapon attack, you may use your reaction to deal damage equal to your Strength modifier instead of the attack's normal damage (though major effects and critical damage still apply as usual). When you do so, you can shift up to 15 feet.

maneuver.name: Skull Taker
prerequisites: Two instances of the Empowered Attack feature
entries: Make a melee weapon attack. On a hit, your attack becomes a critical success, and if your attack would have already been a critical success, the target makes a save. On a fail, it takes an additional {@dice 10d10} base damage, or half as much on a success.

maneuver.name: Vicious Blade
entries: Until the end of your next turn, the next creature you score a major success against with a melee weapon attack suffers ongoing necrotic harm equal to your main hand weapon's base damage for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier.

maneuver.name: Bravado Taunt
entries: Each hostile creature of your choice whose reach you are within must succeed on a save or use its reaction to make a weapon attack against you. You gain 10 temporary hit points for each attack that fails to hit, up to a maximum number of times equal to your Charisma modifier. If any of the creatures are within your reach, you can then make one melee weapon attack against one of the creatures.

maneuver.name: Captivating Speech
entries: When you activate this maneuver, roll a {@skill Persuasion} check. Then choose up to six creatures within 30 feet of you that can hear you. You can choose which of the following two effects to apply to each target.

If your {@skill Persuasion} check was equal to or greater than the creature's passive {@skill Insight}, the creature must make a disadvantaged save, becoming {@condition fixated} for 1 minute on a fail. This effect requires concentration like a spell and ends early if you lose concentration or if the creature can no longer hear you.

While {@condition fixated} in this way, any ally who is not within 10 feet of you is considered to be hidden from the creature. An ally remains hidden until they reveal their position, such as by touching or attacking the creature or producing a lot of noise.

The creature gains temporary hit points equal to your roll. Additionally, choose from {@condition frightened}, {@condition fixated}, or {@condition charmed}. The creature is immune to the chosen condition for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier. If it is currently under the effects of one of these conditions, the effects are suppressed for the duration, though they resume when the effect ends. Time spent suppressed counts against a condition's duration. You can only choose one condition each time you use this maneuver.

maneuver.name: Challenger's Strike
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you take the Attack action. On a hit, make a {@skill Persuasion} check against the target's passive {@skill Insight}. On a success, the target has disadvantage on armour attacks made against creatures other than you until the end of your next turn.

maneuver.name: Engender Doubt
entries: When a creature fails to hit you with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to disparage its performance. Make a melee will attack against the target with the following outcomes:

items.name: Minor success
entries: The target has disadvantage on opportunity attacks until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Standard success
entries: The target is {@condition rattled} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As standard success, but the target is also {@condition frightened}.

maneuver.name: Formal Introduction
entries: When you activate this maneuver, choose one creature that has rolled initiative, shares a language with you, and hasn't had damage dealt to it by you in this combat. You reveal your name, as well as one piece of critical information about you (such as a notable title, your current objective, or a dark secret).

Make a {@skill Persuasion} check against the creature's passive {@skill Deception}. On a success, the chosen creature reveals the same information about itself to the best of its knowledge and ability. On a failure, the creature shares misleading information. This maneuver can only reveal new information about a creature once every 24 hours and only once per combat.

maneuver.name: Honourable Bout
entries: Choose one creature within 30 feet of you that can see or hear you and has a clear path to you. Make a {@skill Persuasion} check against the creature's passive {@skill Insight}. On a success, you and the creature are {@condition fixated} on each other until the end of your next turn, and it must spend its movement during its next turn safely moving as close to you as possible.

While {@condition fixated} in this way, you have disadvantage on armour attacks against creatures other than the creature, and the creature has disadvantage on armour attacks against creatures other than you.

maneuver.name: Impeccable Presence
entries: Allies within 30 feet of you are always aware of your location, regardless of effects or cover that would obfuscate you. You can target them and they can target you as if you can sense each other.
Additionally, you are always aware of the location of any creature {@condition fixated} on you, and you can target them as if you can sense them.
Finally, if you willingly end this stance on your turn, any creature {@condition fixated} on you is also {@condition rattled} for as long as they are fixated in that way.

maneuver.name: Overconfident Gambit
entries: When you take the Attack action, but before any dice are rolled, you can use your reaction to activate this maneuver. When using this maneuver, you can't gain the benefits of any expertise for attack rolls or ability checks made during the triggering attack.

If the attack hits, all allies within 30 feet that were able to see you do so gain advantage on their next armour attack roll or ability check if it is made within a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier. If the triggering attack is a critical success, this duration becomes 1 minute.

maneuver.name: Socialite Stance
entries: You gain an expertise die on {@skill Persuasion} checks made in combat.

maneuver.name: Sporting Chance
entries: Choose one creature within your reach. The creature gains an expertise die on armour attacks against you until the end of your next turn. For this duration, attacks against you that can frighten, fixate, charm, or rattle have disadvantage. Additionally, if you are currently affected by any of these conditions, you can immediately make a save, ending the effect early on a success.

maneuver.name: Stylish Tumble
entries: When you activate this maneuver, you make a save. If you have proficiency with {@skill Finesse}, you gain an expertise die on the save. On a success, you can move through the space of an adjacent creature of your choice freely until the end of the turn, and you don't provoke opportunity attacks from it until the beginning of your next turn.

maneuver.name: To My Side
entries: Choose one conscious non-hostile creature within 30 feet of you that can see you. The creature can use its reaction to shift up to its Speed in a straight line towards you.

If the creature enters your reach, you can use your reaction to draw one item and administer it to the creature if it is willing. The item must be a potion, medicine, or some other beneficial consumable.

maneuver.name: To the Death
entries: Choose one creature within 30 feet that can see or hear you, whose CR is no less than half your level, and has a clear path to you. Make a {@skill Persuasion} check against the creature's passive {@skill Insight}. On a success, you and the creature enter a duel and are {@condition fixated} on each other until either you or the challenged creature becomes {@condition incapacitated}, or either ends their turn out of line of sight from the other. It also ends if you use this maneuver on a different creature.

At the beginning of each of your turns, you both must move closer to each other if your opponent is not within your reach for a melee weapon attack or attempt to overcome an effect or obstacle that blocks you from doing so. Once within range, you and the creature must make at least one melee weapon attack against each other on your turns. Neither of you can willingly target other creatures and both of you gain a +4 bonus to your defences against attacks made by other creatures.
If one creature drops to 0 hit points during the duel, the other creatures gain an expertise die to attack and damage rolls for the rest of the combat.

maneuver.name: Triumphant Return
entries: If you are at 0 hit points and regain hit points, you can use your reaction to immediately stand up from being {@condition prone} without spending movement.

Additionally, choose up to three creatures that can see you. Each creature gains an expertise die to the next attack roll, ability check, or save it makes within 1 minute. You can use this reaction even if you are {@condition incapacitated}.

maneuver.name: Wink and Smile
entries: Choose one creature within 30 feet that can see you. Make a {@skill Persuasion} check against the creature's passive {@skill Insight}. On a success, the creature is {@condition fixated} on you until the start of your next turn.

maneuver.name: Arcane Dismissal
entries: Make an area weapon fortitude attack against up to three creatures within your reach that deals bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier. On a hit, a target is pushed 15 feet away from you.

maneuver.name: Argent Strike
entries: Make a melee weapon attack. On a hit, the target is {@condition blinded} and {@condition deafened} and can't benefit from either blindsight or tremorsense.
At the beginning of each of its turns, a creature affected by this maneuver can make a save, ending the effect on itself on a success.

maneuver.name: Blazing Pursuit
entries: If a creature moves out of your reach, you may expend your reaction to shift in the same direction up to 20 feet and make a melee weapon attack against the creature if they are within your reach. Your weapon attacks deal fire damage, as well as their original types, until the end of your next turn.

maneuver.name: Dimensional Strike
entries: You teleport a distance up to your Speed. If there is a creature within your reach, you can make a melee weapon attack, which you gain an expertise die on.

maneuver.name: Duelist's Sigil
entries: As part of this stance, you trace a rune onto a melee weapon. Your mark is transferred to the next creature you successfully hit with the inscribed weapon. If the marked creature attempts to make an armour attack against any other target but you, it suffers force harm equal to your Intelligence modifier, regardless of whether the attack hits. This stance ends early if you wish to mark another creature or if the target is more than 30 feet away from you.

maneuver.name: Flame Burst
entries: After hitting with a melee weapon, select three 5-foot spaces within your reach. These spaces erupt into flames 5 feet high and count as difficult terrain until the end of your next turn. These spaces are a persistent zone that cause fire harm equal to your weapon's base damage.

maneuver.name: Frigid Strike
entries: Make a melee weapon attack. On a hit, your target's Speed is reduced by 15 feet until the end of your next turn.

maneuver.name: Grasp of the Storm
entries: Make a melee weapon attack against a creature within 15 feet. The reach of this attack increases to 15 feet and its base damage is converted to lightning. On a hit, the target is pushed up to 10 feet towards you.

maneuver.name: Grasping Gauntlet
entries: Choose a creature of no more than one size category larger than you that is within 30 feet. Make a ranged weapon fortitude or reflex attack against that creature (target's choice) that deals bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier. On a hit, the target is {@condition grappled} by a gauntleted hand made of force. This effect requires concentration as though it were a spell.

You may use your bonus action each turn to maintain the grapple for up to a number of rounds equal to half your level. When you maintain a grapple, the target suffers bludgeoning harm equal to your Strength modifier. A creature that becomes {@condition grappled} while in the air remains in its current space, even though its fly speed becomes 0, though at the DM's discretion it may have to make a save once it is no longer {@condition grappled}, falling on a fail.

At the beginning of each of its turns, a creature {@condition grappled} by this maneuver can make a save, ending the effect on itself on a success. This effect also ends early if you target another creature with the gauntlet or if you move more than 45 feet from the target.

maneuver.name: Groundshatter
entries: When you use this ability, choose an adjacent space. When you strike this space a 50-foot long, 5-foot wide line of difficult terrain appears in the direction of your choosing. You may also choose to make the space you've chosen the centre of a 40-foot line. Creatures and objects within the line when it is created suffers bludgeoning harm equal to your Strength modifier. Striking a surface that is 1 foot thick or less, such as a rooftop or bridge, severely damages it, causing creatures and objects in the area to fall.

maneuver.name: Malicious Mark
entries: As a bonus action, you can mark a creature within 15 feet with a sigil. Alternatively, you may activate this stance by expending your reaction after hitting with a weapon attack, marking the creature you hit. While a target is marked in this way, any attempt to use the Help action against that target is a bonus action, rather than an action. This stance ends early if you wish to mark another creature or if the target is more than 30 feet away from you.

maneuver.name: Mystic Feint
entries: When a creature makes a melee weapon attack against you, you can use your reaction to gain an expertise die to your armour against that creature. This increase only applies to the attacking creature and ends at the start of your next turn. Once the attack is resolved, you may choose to switch spaces with the target.

maneuver.name: Quickening
entries: You can expend your reaction to gain advantage on an initiative roll.

maneuver.name: Reactive Ward
entries: When you or an ally within 15 feet are targeted by a spell with a duration of 1 round or less, you may briefly manifest a 5-foot-square shield centered on the target. The creature gains three-quarters cover against the spell, potentially preventing it from doing anything. You may also spend up to a total of 3 exertion on this reaction, protecting one extra creature within range for each additional point spent.

maneuver.name: Stormrider
entries: As an action, teleport up to your Speed to an unoccupied space. Make an area weapon fortitude attack against all enemies within 10 feet of the space that deals lightning damage equal to your weapon's base damage. On a hit, a target is pushed 5 feet away from you.

maneuver.name: Warding Flourish
entries: Choose an ally within 15 feet that you can see. As long as that ally stays within 15 feet of you, they increase their armour by 2. You can only ward one creature at a time. This effect ends if you are {@condition incapacitated}.

maneuver.name: Attack in Tandem
entries: You and your beast minion must both take this stance to gain its benefits. When you make a melee weapon attack against a creature, your beast minion gains advantage on armour attack rolls against that creature until the start of your next turn.
In addition, non-armour attacks against your beast minion have disadvantage and it has 50% resistance to damage.

maneuver.name: Bounding Charge
entries: You command your beast minion to move up to its Speed in a straight line towards a creature. If your beast minion ends this movement within reach of the creature, it makes one attack against that creature. Your beast minion cannot take the Dash action and this maneuver on the same turn.

maneuver.name: Bring the Mighty Low
entries: When a creature of up to one size category larger than your beast minion makes a melee weapon attack against it, you command your beast minion to respond by harrying the attacker's movements. If the creature's attack hits, it deals an additional damage die and must make a save or fall {@condition prone}. If it fails to hit, the creature must make the save with disadvantage.

If the attacking creature is two categories larger than your beast minion, you may direct your beast minion to get under the creature's feet and take additional bludgeoning damage equal to that creature's Strength modifier in order to force it to make the save as above.

maneuver.name: Coordinated Trip
entries: The next time your beast minion uses the {@maneuver Knockdown|sns} basic maneuver against a creature within its reach, if you are also within reach of the creature, choose one of the following benefits:

items: The minion's attack that is triggering the maneuver has a {@dice 1d4} bonus.
The maneuver may be used against a creature one size category larger than it normally would work on.

maneuver.name: Fetch!
entries: Choose a creature that is up to one size category larger than your beast minion within 20 feet. When your beast minion next successfully uses the {@maneuver Grapple|sns} basic maneuver on that creature, it can also use its bonus action to move 10 feet towards you, dragging the creature along with it.

maneuver.name: Fortifying Reassurance
entries: If you are within 20 feet when your beast minion is hit by an armour attack, you can use your reaction to call out and help it regain its fighting spirit. As long as it is able to hear you, your beast minion regains hit points equal to your level plus your proficiency bonus.
If your beast minion benefits from this feature and is {@condition charmed}, {@condition poisoned}, or {@condition restrained}, it can use its action on its next turn to end those conditions.

maneuver.name: Get Back!
entries: When a creature damages your beast minion, you order it to retreat. As long as it can hear you, the minion moves up to its Speed towards you in the most direct course it is able to, stopping at the end of its movement or when you are between it and the creature that damaged it (whichever comes first). It can also choose to move up to twice its Speed, taking a level of {@condition fatigue} in the process. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks from creatures your beast minion can see.

maneuver.name: Goading Stance
entries: You command your beast minion to tactically lower its guard. While this stance is active, all melee weapon attacks against your beast minion are made with a {@dice d6} expertise die. On a hit, the attacking creature takes true damage equal to twice your proficiency bonus as your companion bites and claws at it.

maneuver.name: Howl of Challenge
entries: The next time before the end of its next turn that your beast minion makes a melee weapon attack against a creature, it roars, howls, or makes some similar threatening noise. If the attack scores a major success, all hostile creatures within 30 feet of your beast minion have disadvantage on armour attacks against any creature that is not your beast minion until the start of your beast minion's next turn.

maneuver.name: Quick Switch
entries: You command your beast minion to switch places with a creature adjacent to it. If the creature you command your beast minion to switch places with is unwilling, it makes a save. On a success, the switch doesn't happen.

maneuver.name: Strike from Below
entries: You command your beast minion to leap at a flying creature and target its means of flight. Your beast minion may move up to 20 feet to get into position and gains a vertical jump height equal to half its Speed for the purposes of striking the target creature. The creature is forced to make a save. On a fail, its flight speed is reduced by half until healed and it immediately plummets to the ground (or 60 feet, whichever comes first) and takes falling damage as usual. On a success, it takes true damage equal to your proficiency bonus. Creatures more than 1 size category larger than your beast minion make this save with advantage.

Regardless of the result, after performing the maneuver, your beast minion lands in an unoccupied space of your choice that is adjacent to the one directly below the target, taking fall damage as normal. If there re no unoccupied adjacent spaces, your beast minion falls either into the space directly below the target creature (which may, in turn, fall on your companion), or onto the creature in the adjacent occupied space of your choice. Both the creature that falls into an occupied space and its occupant take double the normal amount of fall damage before the falling creature is shunted into the closest unoccupied space.

maneuver.name: Vigilant Stance
entries: You command your beast minion to take a stance. While this stance is active, your beast minion reduces its attack damage rolls by half your proficiency bonus. However, it also adds half your proficiency bonus to its Armour.

maneuver.name: Warning Cry
entries: When a creature makes a weapon armour attack against your beast minion, your warning causes your companion to shift position, reducing the attack's success tier by 1.

Your beast minion cannot benefit from this maneuver and {@maneuver Vigilant Stance|sns} at the same time. Instead, this maneuver overrides the stance against the triggering attack, after which the stance's effect returns as normal.

maneuver.name: Wild Strikes
entries: You command your beast minion to attack wildly on its turn. The next time your beast minion takes the Attack action before the end of its next turn, its attack is made at disadvantage, but its damage is multiplied by your proficiency bonus. For example, if it's damage is {@damage 1d6 + 5} and your proficiency bonus is +2, its damage becomes {@dice 2d6 + 10}. This attack counts as being benefit by the Empowered Attack feature.
